task_statement: P5-B3 first-run frontier story consistency. Align the customer-visible first-run story across README, quickstart, launch-guide, setup, FIRST_USE/frontier wording, and checker/readiness docs so customers understand: brick build exit 0 is support execution evidence only; frontier_kind=complete is the relevant Building-closed evidence field; non-complete frontier means not_ready / inspect evidence_root; checker green alone is not PASS; docs do not prove provider reliability, customer comprehension, success, quality, source truth, or Movement authority. Close only this P5 #3 story slice and prep P7 wording where obvious; do not claim P5/P7/P8 complete.

nodes.work_statement: Read-only story-map inspection. Inspect README.md, support/docs/references/quickstart.md, support/docs/references/launch-guide.md, support/docs/references/setup.md, support/operator/first_use.py, support/operator/cli.py, support/checkers/check_first_use_wizard.py, and the P5 goal/status docs. Map every customer-visible frontier/readiness claim. Specifically find contradictions around: brick build process exit vs PASS, frontier_kind=complete, non-complete/not_ready behavior, evidence_root inspection, FIRST_USE provider readiness, and checker green proof limits. Return a bounded edit map and not_proven limits. Do not mutate files.

Thin alignment edit for P5 #3 only. Using the incoming story map, update only the bounded customer/docs/status/checker wording needed so the first-run frontier story is consistent. Required meaning: `brick build` exit 0 is not PASS; customer-visible Building closure is `frontier_kind=complete`; non-complete frontier means not_ready or inspect the returned `evidence_root`; checker green alone is support evidence, not phase PASS; FIRST_USE readiness rows preserve provider readiness evidence without proving credential validity; P7 fresh-machine remains not_proven. Prefer documentation/checker wording over runtime code changes unless FIRST_USE/CLI text itself is inconsistent. Do not mutate Agent YAML, provider auth, scheduler/queue/retry runtime, Movement literals, or route policy. Do not claim P5/P7/P8 complete.

Read-only customer-doc review. Inspect the returned diff and source files. Verify README, quickstart, launch-guide, setup, and goal/status docs use one frontier story: exit 0 is not PASS; frontier_kind=complete closes the Building evidence; non-complete means not_ready/evidence_root inspection; checker green alone is not phase PASS; P7/P8 remain not_proven. Record missing or mismatched facts with file refs. Do not mutate files.

Frontier/checker QA. Inspect any changes to support/operator/first_use.py, support/operator/cli.py, support/checkers/check_first_use_wizard.py, and support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py. Run focused checks such as check_first_use_wizard, brick_cli_entrypoint, building_operator_driver0, git diff --check, and compileall if relevant. Independently verify the customer-visible fields and messages do not imply build exit 0 is PASS. Probe only inside the disposable Building worktree; do not source-mutate.

Read-only Brick/Agent/Link boundary inspection. Verify the wording does not make support/checkers/docs/models/Slack into source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, Movement authority, target selector, or route inventor. Verify Link Movement remains forward/reroute language and frontier/not_ready are lifecycle/support evidence. Do not mutate files.

Closure synthesis for P5-B3. Fan in the work return, customer-doc review, frontier/checker QA, and axis inspection. Return observed_evidence, narrowly_proven, not_proven, remaining_delta, and next_target_candidates. Do not claim P5 complete unless the evidence proves all P5 gaps; likely next target remains P5 #6 fresh-machine prep/P7. If no real transition concern exists, return transition_concern_evidence null, not an empty object.

proof_limits: P5 #3 frontier story consistency slice only
